questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $a, b, c$ are first three terms of a G.P. if the harmonic mean of $a$ and $b$ is 12 and arithmetic mean of $b$ and $c$ is 3 , then
options: no term of this G.P. is square of an integer
arithmetic mean of $a, b, c$ is 3
common ratio of this G.P. is 2
questions.solution:
$a, b, c$ are in GP
$\frac{2 a b}{a+b}=12, \frac{b+c}{2}=3$
$\frac{2 a \cdot a r}{a+a r}=12, a r+a r^2=6$
$a r=6(1+r), \operatorname{ar}(1+r)=6$
i.e. $6(1+r)^2=6$, i.e. $(1+r)^2=1$
$\therefore 1+r= \pm 1$ i.e. $r=0$ or $r=-2$
$\therefore \mathrm{r}=-2$
If $r=-2$, then $a=3$
$\therefore$ The GP is $3,-6,12,-24,48$
Clearly no term of the series is square of an integer AM of $a, b, c$ is $=\frac{3-6+12}{3}=3$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The sum of the first three numbers in an A.P. is 24 and their product is 384. Then which of the following hold(s) good?
options: sum of n terms can be 2n(n + 1)
sum to n terms can be 2n(n + 3)
sum of $n$ terms can be $14 n-2 n^2$
sum of the squares of the three numbers is 224
questions.solution:
Let the number be $(a-d), a,(a+d)$
$3 a=24$
$a=8$
Also $(8-d)(8)(8+d)=384$
$\begin{aligned}
& d^2=16 \\
& d=4 \text { or }-4
\end{aligned}$
Hence series is $4,8,12, \ldots$.
$\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow S_n=2 n(n+1) \text { or } 12,8,4, \ldots \ldots \\
& \Rightarrow S_n=14 n-2 n^2
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Given that $\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{y}+\mathrm{z}=15$ when $\mathrm{a}, \mathrm{x}, \mathrm{y}, \mathrm{z}, \mathrm{b}$ are in A.P. and $\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}}+\frac{1}{\mathrm{y}}+\frac{1}{\mathrm{z}}=\frac{5}{3}$ when $\mathrm{a}, \mathrm{x}, \mathrm{y}, \mathrm{z}, \mathrm{b}$ are in H.P. then
options: one possible value of $a+2 b$ is 11
A.M. of a and b is 6
questions.solution: $x+y+z=3\left(\frac{a+b}{2}\right) ; \frac{1}{x}+\frac{1}{y}+\frac{1}{z}=\frac{3\left(\frac{1}{a}+\frac{1}{b}\right)}{2}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: $a, b, c$ are the first three terms of geometric series. If the H.M. of $a$ and $b$ is 12 and that of $b$ and $c$ is 36 then which of the following hold(s) good?
options: Sum of the first term and common ratio of the G.P. is 11.
Sum of the first five terms of the G.P. is 948 .
If the value of the the first term and common ratio of the given G.P. is taken as the first term and common difference of an A.P. then its $8^{\text {th }}$ term is 29.
The number 648 is one of the term of the G.P.
questions.solution:
$a, b, c$ are in G.P.; $b=a r, \quad c=a r^2$
$12=\frac{2 a b}{a+b} \Rightarrow \frac{a b}{a+b}=6 \Rightarrow \frac{a^2 r}{a(1+r)}=6 \Rightarrow \frac{a r}{1+r}=6$.....(1)
again $36=\frac{2 b c}{b+c} \Rightarrow \frac{b c}{b+c}=18$
$\frac{\mathrm{a}^2 \mathrm{r}^3}{\operatorname{ar}(1+\mathrm{r})}=18 \Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{ar}^2}{1+\mathrm{r}}=18$.....(2)
from (1) and (2), a = 8, r=3 now verify all the alternatives
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $0 \lt \theta \lt \frac{\pi}{4}$. Then,
questions.solution:
Since $0 \lt \theta \lt \frac{\pi}{4}, 0 \lt \sin ^2 \theta, \cos ^2 \theta, \tan ^2 \theta \lt 1$
(A) $\sum_{\mathrm{k}=0}^{\infty} \sin ^{2 \mathrm{k}} \theta=\frac{1}{1-\sin ^2 \theta}=\sec ^2 \theta$
(B) $\sum_{\mathrm{k}=0}^{\infty} \cos ^{2 \mathrm{k}} \theta=\frac{1}{1-\cos ^2 \theta}=\operatorname{cosec}^2 \theta$
(C) $\sum_{\mathrm{k}=0}^{\infty} \tan ^{2 \mathrm{k}} \theta=\frac{1}{1-\tan ^2 \theta}=\frac{\cos ^2 \theta}{\cos ^2 \theta-\sin ^2 \theta}=\frac{\cos ^2 \theta}{\cos 2 \theta}$
(D) $\cot \theta\gt1$ in $0 \lt \theta \lt \frac{\pi}{4}$. Hence, $\sum_{\mathrm{k}=0}^{\infty} \cot ^{2 \mathrm{k}} \theta$ does not exist.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The first term of an infinite geometric series is 21 . The second term and the sum of the series are both positive integers. The possible value(s) of the second term can be
questions.solution:
Let the series be $21,21 \mathrm{r}, 21 \mathrm{r}^2, \ldots \ldots$.
Sum $=\frac{21}{1-r}$ is a positive integer
also 21 r is a positive integer
$\mathrm{S}=\frac{(21)(21)}{21-21 \mathrm{r}}$ as $21 \mathrm{r} \in \mathrm{N}$ hence $21-21 \mathrm{r}$ must be an integer
also $21 \mathrm{r} \lt 21$
Hence 21 - 21r may be equal to 1, 3, 7 or 9 (think!) i.e. must be a divisor of (21)(21)

Hence, $21-21 \mathrm{r}=1$ or 3 or 7 or 9
$21 r=20,18,14 \text { or } 12$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A triangle with side lengths of $a, b$ and $c$ is a right triangle where $a < b < c$. Which of the following statements are possible?
questions.solution:
(a)  $a, a+d, a+2 d$
$\begin{aligned} & a^2+(a+d)^2=(a+2 d)^2 \\ & 2 a^2+d^2+2 a d=a^2+4 d^2+4 a d \\ & a^2-2 a d=3 d^2 \\ & \left(\frac{a}{d}\right)^2-\frac{2 a}{3}=3\end{aligned}$
$t^2-2 t-3=0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad(t-3)(t+1)=0 \Rightarrow t=3$
$a=3 d \quad$ [possible]
(b) $\quad a=a ; b=a r ; c=a r^2$
$\begin{aligned} & c^2=a^2+b^2 \\ & a^2 r^4=a^2\left(1+r^2\right)\end{aligned}$
$r^2 t^2-t-1=0$
$\begin{array}{ll}r^2=\frac{1+\sqrt{5}}{2}>1 \quad \text { [possible] }\end{array}$
$\begin{aligned} & a, b, c \rightarrow \text { A.P. } \\ & b=\frac{2 a c}{a+c} \\ & c^2=a^2+\frac{4 a^2 c^2}{(a+c)^2} \\ & a^4+2 a^3 c+4 a^2 c^2-2 a c^3-c^4=0\end{aligned}$
Divide by $a^2 c^2$
$\begin{aligned} & \left(\frac{a^2}{c^2}-\frac{c^2}{a^2}\right)+2\left(\frac{a}{c}-\frac{c}{a}\right)+4=0 \\ & \frac{a}{c}-\frac{c}{a}=t ; t < 0 \\ & t\left(\frac{a}{c}-\frac{c}{a}\right)+2 t+4=0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad t \sqrt{t^2+4}+2 t+4=0, t < 0\end{aligned}$
$\begin{array}{llll}\Rightarrow & t^2\left(t^2+4\right)=4(t+2)^2 & & \\ \Rightarrow & f(t)=t^4-16 t-16=0 & \Rightarrow & f(0)=-16 \\ \Rightarrow & f^{\prime}=4 t^3-4 & \Rightarrow & f^{\prime \prime}=12 t^2>0\end{array}$
One negative, one positive root.
$\therefore \quad \frac{a}{c}-\frac{c}{a}$ has positive roots.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
If $a_1, a_2, \ldots \ldots, a_n$ is a sequence of positive numbers which are in A.P. with common difference $d$ and $a_1+a_4+a_7+\ldots \ldots .+a_{16}=147$ 
then $a_1+a_{16}=M$ and $a_1+a_6+a_{11}+a_{16}=N$
Maximum value of $a_1 a_2 \ldots \ldots . a_{16}=\left(\frac{S}{W}\right)^{16}$ (where $S$ and $W$ are coprime), then:
questions.solution:
Using, A.M. $\geq$ G.M.
$\frac{\sum a_i}{16} \geq\left(a_1 a_2 \ldots \ldots a_{16}\right)^{1 / 16}$
$\therefore \quad a_1 a_2 \ldots \ldots a_{16} \leq\left(\frac{a_1+a_2+\ldots \ldots a_{16}}{16}\right)^{16} \leq\left(\frac{392}{16}\right)^{16} \leq\left(\frac{49}{2}\right)^{16}$
$\Rightarrow \quad S=49$ and $W=2$
$\frac{6}{2}(2 a+5 \cdot 3 d)=147$
$2 a+15 d=49$  ...(1)
$2 a+15 d=M=49$
Also,$\frac{4}{2}(2 a+3 \cdot 5 d)=N \quad ; \quad(2 a+15 d) 2=N \quad \Rightarrow \quad N=98$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $a_1, a_2, a_3 \ldots \ldots$ and $b_1, b_2, b_3 \ldots \ldots$ be arithmetic progressions such that $a_1=25, b_1=75$ and $a_{100}+b_{100}=100$. Then
options: The difference between successive terms in progression 'a' is opposite of the difference in progression 'b'.
questions.solution:
$\begin{array}{ll}
a_1+\left(a_1+d\right), & \left(a_1+2 d\right), \\
b_1+\left(b_1+d_1\right), & \left(b_1+2 d_1\right)
\end{array}$
hence $a_{100}=a_1+99 d$
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{b}_{100}=\mathrm{b}_1+99 \mathrm{~d}_1 \\
& \text { add }
\end{aligned}$
$a_{100}+b_{100}=100+99\left(d+d_1\right)$
hence $d+d_1=0$
$\Rightarrow d=-d_1 \Rightarrow(A)$
(B) and (C) are obviously true.
$\sum_{r=1}^{100}\left(a_r+b_r\right)=\frac{100}{2}\left[\left(a_1+b_1\right)+\left(a_{100}+b_{100}\right)\right]=\frac{100 \times 200}{2}=10^4 \Rightarrow(D)\left(\text { using } S_n=\frac{n}{2}(a+d)\right)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(n)=\sum_{r=1}^n \log _{10}\left(\frac{9 r+1}{9 r-8}\right)$, then:
questions.solution:
$f(n)=\sum_{r=1}^n\left(\log _{10}(9 r+1)-\log _{10}(9 r-8)\right)$
$\therefore \quad f(n)=\log _{10}(9 n+1)$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $x_1, x_2, x_3, x_4$ are four positive real numbers such that $x_1+\frac{1}{x_2}=4, x_2+\frac{1}{x_3}=1, x_3+\frac{1}{x_4}=4$ and $x_4+\frac{1}{x_1}=1$, then
questions.solution:
$4=x_1+\frac{1}{x_2} \geq 2 \sqrt{\frac{x_1}{x_2}}$
$1=x_2+\frac{1}{x_3} \geq 2 \sqrt{\frac{x_2}{x_3}}$
$4=x_3+\frac{1}{x_4} \geq 2 \sqrt{\frac{x_3}{x_4}}$
$1=x_4+\frac{1}{x_1} \geq 2 \sqrt{\frac{x_4}{x_1}}$
$16=\left(x_1+\frac{1}{x_2}\right)\left(x_2+\frac{1}{x_3}\right)\left(x_3+\frac{1}{x_4}\right)\left(x_4+\frac{1}{x_1}\right) \geq 2^4$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If sum of an infinite G.P. is $p(p \in R)$, then which of the following can be the common ratio of the G.P.?
questions.solution:
For the sum of infinite G.P. to be a finite quantity, common ratio $r$ must satisfy $0 < |r| < 1$
$\therefore \quad$ Options (b) and (d) correct.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $n^{\text {th }}$ term of the series $6+17+34+57+\ldots \ldots$. is $\left(\log _2 a\right) n^2+\left(\log _3(b-a)\right)_n+\log _4 c$, then:
questions.solution:
$S_n=6+17+34+57+\ldots \ldots+T_n$
$\operatorname{Sub} \frac{S_n=6+17+34+57+\ldots \ldots+T_{n-1}+T_n}{0=6+11+17+23+\ldots \ldots+\left(T_n-T_{n-1}\right)-T_n}$
$\begin{aligned} & T_n=6+11+17+23+\ldots \ldots+\left(T_n-T_{n-1}\right)=6+\frac{n-1}{2}(2 \times 11+(n-1-1) 6) \\ & T_n=6+(n-1)(3 n+5) \\ & T_n=3 x^2+2 x+1 \equiv\left(\log _2 a\right) n^2+\left(\log _3(b-a)\right) n+\log _4 c\end{aligned}$
$\begin{array}{ll}\therefore \quad & \log _2 a=3 \Rightarrow a=8 ; \log _3(b-a)=2 \Rightarrow b-a=9 \Rightarrow b=17 \\ & \log _4 c=1 \Rightarrow c=4\end{array}$
Now, verify the options.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $\left(x^2+3 x+2\right),\left(x^2-x-10\right)$ and $\left(x^2+x-4+y^2\right)$ are first three positive terms of an A.P., such that their squares will form a G.P., then which of the following is true?
options: Sum of all possible integral value(s) of $x$ is -3 .
If $y, y+3$ and $z$ are in A.P., then $z$ is equal to 6.
Harmonic mean of given numbers is 2 .
Sum of first 20 terms of this A.P. is 40 .
questions.solution:
$x^2+3 x+2, x^2-x-10$ and $x^2+x-4+y^2$ are in A.P. as well in G.P.
$\begin{array}{ll}\Rightarrow & x^2+3 x+2=x^2-x-10=x^2+x-4+y^2 \\ \Rightarrow & x=-3, y=0\end{array}$
Each number is equal to 2 .
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\sin ^2 \frac{\mathrm{~A}}{2}, \sin ^2 \frac{B}{2}, \sin ^2 \frac{\mathrm{C}}{2}$ are in H.P. then
options: a, b. c are in A.P.
a, b. c are in H.P.
questions.solution:
$\frac{(s-b)(s-c)}{b c}, \frac{(s-a)(s-c)}{a c}, \frac{(s-a)(s-b)}{a b} \quad$ H.P.
$\Rightarrow \frac{a}{(s-a)} \cdot \frac{b}{(s-b)}, \frac{c}{(s-c)}$    H.P.
$\Rightarrow \frac{(s-a)}{a}, \frac{(s-b)}{b}, \frac{(s-c)}{c}$  A.P.
$\Rightarrow \frac{s}{a}-1, \frac{s}{b}-1, \frac{s}{c}-1$    A.P.
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b}, \mathrm{c}$    H.P.
$\frac{1}{a}, \frac{1}{b}, \frac{1}{c}$  A.P.
$-\frac{1}{a},-\frac{1}{b},-\frac{1}{c}$  A.P.
$\frac{1}{b}+\frac{1}{c}, \frac{1}{c}+\frac{1}{a} \cdot \frac{1}{a}+\frac{1}{b}$   A.P.
$a(b+c), b(c+a), c(a+b)$    A.P.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Let $ < T_n>$ be a sequence such that $T_n^3+2 T_n=T_{n+1} \forall n \in N$, and $T_1=1$, then:

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $-6 \alpha, \beta$ and $3 \alpha^2+3 \beta$ (in order) are the first three consecutive terms of an A.P. where $\alpha$. and $\beta$ are natural numbers, then:
options: the sum of the first 9 terms is 270 .
the sum of the first 9 terms is 540 .
questions.solution:
$-6 \alpha+3 \alpha^2+3 \beta=2 \beta$
$\begin{aligned} & 3 \alpha^2-6 \alpha+\beta=0 \\ & \because \quad \alpha \text { is real. } \quad \therefore D \geq 0 \\ & 36-12 \beta \geq 0 \quad \Rightarrow \beta \leq 3 \\ & \end{aligned}$
For $\beta=3,3 \alpha^2-6 \alpha+3=0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad \alpha=1$
$\therefore \quad$ A.P. is $-6,3,12, \ldots \ldots \ldots$
Now, verify the options.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $5 \cdot 2^8 \cdot 3^{16}$ is one of the terms of a G.P. whose first term is 5 and all its terms are natural number then possible common ratio of the G.P. is:
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & a=5 \\ & T_{r+1}=5 \cdot r^{n-1}=5 \cdot 2^8 \cdot 3^{16} \\ & r^{n-1}=\left(2 \cdot 3^2\right)^8=\left(\left(2 \cdot 3^2\right)^2\right)^4=\left(\left(2 \cdot 3^2\right)^4\right)^2=\left(\left(2 \cdot 3^2\right)^8\right)^1\end{aligned}$
$\therefore \quad$ Possible common ratio of the G.P. are
$2 \cdot 3^2,\left(2 \cdot 3^2\right)^2,\left(2 \cdot 3^2\right)^4,\left(2 \cdot 3^2\right)^8$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: For $n \geq 1$, Let $G_n$ be the geometric mean of $\left\{\sin \frac{k \pi}{2 n}: 1 \leq k \leq n\right\}$ then $\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} G_n$ equals:
options:
$\lim _{x \rightarrow 0^{-}}\left[\frac{e^x-1}{x}\right]$
[Note: $[k]$ denotes greatest integer function less than or equal to $k$.]
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & G_n=\left(\prod_{k=1}^n \sin \frac{k \pi}{2 n}\right)^{1 / n} \\ & \begin{aligned} \ln \left(G_n\right) & =\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{1}{n} \sum_{k=1}^n \ln \left(\sin \frac{k \pi}{2 n}\right) \\ & =\int_0^1 \ln \left(\sin \frac{\pi x}{2}\right) d x\end{aligned}\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & =\frac{2}{\pi} \int_0^{\pi / 2} \ln (\sin \theta) d \theta=-\ln 2 \\ \therefore \quad \lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} G_n & =\frac{1}{2}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $a, b, c$ (in order) be the first three terms of a sequence and satisfying $\log \left(\frac{8 b^3-a^3-c^3}{6 a b c}\right)=0$ and $\log b=\log \left(a^2-4\right)=\log (c-2)$. If $T_n$ and $S_n$ denote $n^{\text {th }}$ term and sum of first $n$ terms of the sequence, then:
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & 8 b^3-a^3-c^3=6 a b c \\ & a^3+(-2 b)^3+c^3=3 a(-2 b) c\end{aligned}$
$$
\Rightarrow a-2 b+c=0 \Rightarrow a, b, c \text { are in A.P. }
$$
$$
\Rightarrow \quad a=3, b=5, c=7
$$
Now, verify the options.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $A_1, A_2 ; G_1, G_2$ and $H_1, H_2$ be two AM's, GM's and HM's respectively between two positive real numbers $a$ and $b$, then:
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { } A_1=\frac{2 a+b}{3} \quad ; \quad A_2=\frac{a+2 b}{3} \\ & G_1=a^{2 / 3} b^{1 / 3} \quad ; \quad G_2=a^{1 / 3} b^{2 / 3} \\ & H_1=\frac{3 a b}{a+2 b} \quad ; \quad H_2=\frac{3 a b}{2 a+b} \\ & \therefore \quad A_1 H_2=\frac{2 a+b}{3} \cdot \frac{3 a b}{2 a+b}=a b \\ & \therefore \quad G_1 G_2=a b \\ & \therefore \quad A_2 H_1=\frac{a+2 b}{3} \cdot \frac{3 a b}{a+2 b}=a b \\ & \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: For $0 < x < \pi / 2$ if $\sin x,(\sin x+1)$ and $6(\sin x+1)$ are in G.P., then:
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text {  } \quad 2(\sin x+1)^2=\sin x(6)(\sin x+1) \\ & 2 \sin ^2 x+4 \sin x+2=6 \sin ^2 x+6 \sin x \\ & \Rightarrow 4 \sin ^2 x+2 \sin x-2=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad 2 \sin ^2 x+\sin x-1=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad(2 \sin x-1)(\sin x+1)=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \sin x=\frac{1}{2} \quad ; \quad \sin x=-1 \quad \text { (rejected) } \\ & \Rightarrow x=\frac{\pi}{6} \\ & \text { G.P. } \frac{1}{2}, \sqrt{2}\left(\frac{1}{2}+1\right), 6\left(\frac{1}{2}+1\right), \ldots \ldots \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{1}{2}, \frac{3}{\sqrt{2}}, 9, \ldots \ldots \ldots \\ & T_5=a r^4=\frac{1}{2}(3 \sqrt{2})^4=81 \times 2=162 \\ & S_n=\frac{\frac{1}{2}\left[(3 \sqrt{2})^n-1\right]}{3 \sqrt{2}-1} \\ & \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
